title: How the Iran war increases the global nuclear threat | ABC News Daily podcast
description: One of Donald Trump’s stated reasons for going to war with Iran is to destroy the nuclear threat he says it poses.     But by starting the conflict has he made the world less safe?    There are concerns around what happens to Iran’s enriched nuclear material and the lessons other countries will take from Iran’s being hit while remaining at the threshold short of getting a bomb.   Today, Kelsey Davenport from the Arms Control Association on why it could all lead to more nuclear weapons around the world.   Featured:    Kelsey Davenport, Director for Nonproliferation Policy at the Arms Control Association

title: Nepal holds first election since deadly youth-led uprising in 2025 | ABC NEWS
description: Nepal has held its first election since the deadly youth-led protests toppled the government in 2025. Key figures contesting for power include the Marxist former prime minister KP Sharma Oli, seeking a return to office; a rapper-turned-mayor, Balendra Shah, bidding for the youth vote; and the newly elected leader of the powerful Nepali Congress party, Gagan Thapa. title: Trump ‘fully supports’ Kurdish groups joining war against Iran | ABC NEWS
description: Two senior sources in the Kurdish coalition told the ABC they will enter Iran once they have enough US and Israeli air support. Kurds are one of the largest ethnic groups in the world, living in Iran, Iraq, Syria and Turkey. They make up about 10 per cent of Iran's population and have long sought autonomy. US President Donald Trump says he fully supports the Kurds joining the conflict but has not said if the US will provide air support. Former US Deputy National Security Advisor James Jeffrey said the push for Kurdish involvement is a 'propaganda gambit' from the US. Dr Martin Kear from the University of Sydney echoes this sentiment and says it is unrealistic for Kurdish troops to take control of Iran.
